result:
Subject: About yesterday's payments outage — what happened and what we're doing about it

Dear Customer,

Yesterday, for roughly four hours, our payments service was degraded, and during that window some of you saw transactions fail outright. That is a serious failure on our part. Payments are the part of our product you trust us with most, and we did not meet the bar you deserve. I am sorry — both for the disruption itself and for the stress and rework it caused on your side.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule that behaved correctly under normal load but broke down when traffic spiked, causing stale data to be served into the payment path and transactions to be rejected. Our team identified the rule, rolled it back, and fully restored service. We have since added the specific failure mode to our regression suite so this exact issue cannot recur silently.

Preventing the next one matters more than explaining this one. Going forward, configuration changes that touch the payment path will be released through canary rollouts — gradually, with automatic rollback on error-rate regressions — rather than fleet-wide. We are also expanding chaos testing in our staging environment to deliberately simulate traffic spikes and cache failures, so a class of failure like this one is surfaced before it ever reaches you.

If your transaction failed during the incident, we will be applying a goodwill credit to your account automatically within the next few business days — no action required on your part. If you believe you were affected and do not see a credit, please reply to this email and we will make it right. Thank you for your patience, and for continuing to trust us with your business.

Sincerely,
The Payments Team
